story.headline: Washington outlasts Miami in 35-28 battle
body: Washington found just enough in a 35-28 win over Miami in Week 2 PCFL action, with Graham Mertz earning Player of the Game honors after going for 30 of 53 for 389 yards and 3 TDs.
The Huskies were outgained by Miami 404–330 in total offense, including 93 yards on the ground. Miami kept fighting, managing 404 total yards on the night.
Up next, Washington travels to face Oregon in Week 3.

story.headline: Notre Dame steamrolls Arkansas, 35-10
body: Notre Dame dominated from start to finish in a 35-10 rout over Arkansas in Week 2 PCFL action, with Hollywood Smothers earning Player of the Game honors after going for 62 rushing yards and 2 rushing TDs.
The Fighting Irish outgained Arkansas 478–310 in total offense, including 145 yards on the ground. Arkansas kept fighting, managing 310 total yards on the night.
Up next, Notre Dame hosts Georgia in Week 3.

story.headline: Tennessee outlasts Penn State in 26-20 battle
body: Tennessee found just enough in a 26-20 win over Penn State in Week 2 PCFL action, with George MacIntyre earning Player of the Game honors after going for 21 of 28 for 278 yards and 2 TDs.
The Volunteers outgained Penn State 398–281 in total offense, including 89 yards on the ground. Penn State kept fighting, managing 281 total yards on the night.
Up next, Tennessee travels to face Ohio State in Week 3.

story.headline: Colorado takes control late, tops Georgia 42-28
body: Colorado asserted control when it mattered in a 42-28 win over Georgia in Week 2 PCFL action, with Roger Anderson earning Player of the Game honors after going for 24 of 38 for 350 yards and 3 TDs.
The Buffaloes outgained Georgia 462–393 in total offense, including 117 yards on the ground. Georgia kept fighting, managing 393 total yards on the night.
Up next, Colorado hosts Boston College in Week 3.

story.headline: Instant classic: LSU edges Oregon, 27-24
body: LSU found just enough in a 27-24 thriller over Oregon in Week 2 PCFL action, with Garrett Nussmeier earning Player of the Game honors after going for 16 of 29 for 212 yards and 3 TDs.
The Tigers were outgained by Oregon 355–250 in total offense, including 78 yards on the ground. Oregon kept fighting, managing 355 total yards on the night.
Up next, LSU travels to face Michigan in Week 3.

story.headline: Instant classic: Clemson edges Boston College, 21-20
body: Clemson found just enough in a 21-20 thriller over Boston College in Week 2 PCFL action, with James Cortez earning Player of the Game honors after going for 19 of 30 for 252 yards and 1 TD.
The Tigers were outgained by Boston College 405–345 in total offense, including 114 yards on the ground. Boston College kept fighting, managing 405 total yards on the night.
Up next, Clemson hosts Oklahoma in Week 3.

story.headline: Texas pulls away from Ohio State, 29-15
body: Texas asserted control when it mattered in a 29-15 win over Ohio State in Week 2 PCFL action, with Arch Manning earning Player of the Game honors after going for 18 of 29 for 272 yards and 4 TDs.
The Longhorns were outgained by Ohio State 421–370 in total offense, including 107 yards on the ground. Ohio State kept fighting, managing 421 total yards on the night.
Up next, Texas travels to face UCLA in Week 3.

story.headline: Michigan steamrolls UCLA, 51-28
body: Michigan dominated from start to finish in a 51-28 rout over UCLA in Week 2 PCFL action, with Nico Iamaleava earning Player of the Game honors after going for 30 of 46 for 368 yards and 4 TDs.
The Wolverines outgained UCLA 519–479 in total offense, including 183 yards on the ground. UCLA kept fighting, managing 479 total yards on the night.
Up next, Michigan hosts LSU in Week 3.

story.headline: USC survives Oklahoma by 3 in thriller
body: USC found just enough in a 35-32 thriller over Oklahoma in Week 2 PCFL action, with Arnold Glass earning Player of the Game honors after going for 30 of 46 for 388 yards and 3 TDs.
The Trojans outgained Oklahoma 526–445 in total offense, including 91 yards on the ground. Oklahoma kept fighting, managing 445 total yards on the night.
Up next, USC travels to face Miami in Week 3.
